scenario: A complex onboarding form needs rich-text static content between fields -- section headers in bold, bulleted lists of instructions, hyperlinks to policy docs. The team wants to use Markdown for the rich text.
question: Which Forms component supports Markdown rendering for static rich-text content?

options.text: Camunda Forms supports only plain text display -- there is no Markdown, bold, italic, headers, links, or any other formatting available for static content between form fields. If teams need rich text, they must implement a custom renderer outside the standard form schema. This claim is inaccurate. The Camunda Forms element library includes a Text view component that explicitly renders Markdown content, including GitHub-flavored Markdown syntax for headers, lists, bold/italic text, hyperlinks, and blockquotes. The Markdown rendering capability has been part of the Forms element library since its early versions.

Embed raw HTML markup directly into a form text field -- for example, using <strong>, <ul>, <li>, and <a> tags to produce bold text, lists, and links. While HTML can technically express the same formatting, the Camunda Forms design deliberately abstracts above HTML. This enables the form schema to render correctly across multiple surfaces: Tasklist's web UI, embedded React apps, mobile clients, and future renderers. Raw HTML embedding bypasses this abstraction and creates renderer-specific coupling. The Markdown-based Text view component is the portable, renderer-agnostic way to add rich static content.

}}; after evaluation the result is rendered as Markdown supporting GitHub flavored Markdown plus a small safe HTML subset (per :13). There is no separate "Heading" / "Paragraph" element; Markdown headings (#, ##) and paragraphs are produced inside the Text view content itself.

Embed an <iframe> element in the form pointing to an external service that renders Markdown -- for example, a separate web page that converts Markdown to HTML and displays it. This approach is significantly over-engineered for a use case that the built-in Text view component handles natively. iframes introduce cross-origin complications, break form layout flow, prevent the Markdown content from inheriting the form's styling and theme, and require maintaining an additional external service. The Text view component renders Markdown directly inside the form without any external dependency.

a.text: Incorrect. Camunda Forms' static-text component is Text view (singular -- there is no separate Heading element per the element-library list). It accepts Markdown / FEEL / templating per forms-element-library-text.md:13, renders as HTML at display time, supports GitHub-flavored Markdown (# headings, lists, bold, italic, hyperlinks, blockquotes, etc.) plus a small safe HTML subset. For heavier HTML rendering needs the docs recommend the dedicated HTML component (per :67). This is the canonical way to embed rich documentation/help text in a form.

b.text: Incorrect. Markdown support is documented in the Forms element library.

c.text: Correct. Forms abstract above HTML for portability across renderers. Raw HTML embedding isn't the form-spec way; Markdown is.

d.text: Incorrect. iframes for static text inside a form would be wasteful and break form integration.

explanation: Forms abstract above HTML for portability across renderers. Raw HTML embedding isn't the form-spec way; Markdown is.
